---
title: "Run and build the Trial-Termination Early-Warning console"
subtitle: "The R Shiny console, from running it to building it yourself"
format:
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: On this page
    embed-resources: true
    highlight-style: github-dark
    code-copy: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Run it

```r
install.packages(c("shiny", "bslib", "DT"))   # once
shiny::runApp("risk_monitor/shiny")
```

Select a signal, set its level, and watch the tier and overall risk re-colour.

## The one principle: separate the logic from the UI

The roll-up lives in plain functions in `shiny/R/logic.R`. The app, the
[visual guide](risk-guide.html), the [dashboard](risk-dashboard.html), and the unit
tests all call the same functions.

## Step 1 - the transparent roll-up

```r
SEVERITY <- c(green = 0, amber = 50, red = 100)

tier_score <- function(signals, tier) {
  x <- signals[signals$tier == tier, ]
  sev <- SEVERITY[as.character(x$level)]
  score <- sum(sev * x$weight) / sum(x$weight)     # severity-weighted blend
  list(score = round(score), rag = rag_of(score))
}

risk_overall <- function(signals) {                 # weight the tiers
  ts <- vapply(TIERS, function(t) tier_score(signals, t)$score, numeric(1))
  w  <- c(Participant = .5, Study = .3, Client = .2)[TIERS]
  round(sum(ts * w) / sum(w))
}
```

Three levels, per-signal weights, tier and overall blends, RAG thresholds. Change a
weight or a threshold here and every surface updates.

## Step 2 - the data is a signal feed you can swap

`data/signals.csv` carries `tier, id, signal, category, engine, level, weight,
advanced, detail`. It is a representative subset of the 65-signal catalog; replace
it with your live KRI / stopping-rule feed (same columns). `advanced = TRUE` marks
the continuous signals from the published-method engine (Optimal Transport,
Active Inference).

```r
s <- risk_load("data")
risk_overall(s)$rag          # the portfolio RAG
risk_feed(s)                 # every firing signal, ranked red-first
```

## Step 3 - the app skeleton and the what-if

```r
ui <- page_sidebar(
  title = "Trial-Termination Early-Warning",
  theme = bs_theme(version = 5, bootswatch = "darkly"),
  sidebar = sidebar(
    selectInput("tier", "Show tier", c("All", TIERS)),
    selectInput("sig", "Signal", ids),
    radioButtons("level", NULL, c("green","amber","red"), inline = TRUE)),
  # value boxes (overall + 3 tiers), signals table, feed ...
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  rv <- reactiveValues(signals = seed)
  observeEvent(input$level, {                       # the what-if
    i <- which(rv$signals$id == input$sig)
    rv$signals$level[i] <- factor(input$level, levels = c("green","amber","red"))
  })
  ov <- reactive(risk_overall(rv$signals))          # recomputes; boxes/feed read it
}
```

Setting a signal's level writes to `rv$signals`, which re-runs `ov()`, which
re-colours the tier boxes and reorders the feed.

## Step 4 - the value boxes and feed

Colour each value box by its RAG (`renderUI` returning a coloured span), render the
signals as an editable-select `DT`, and build the feed as ranked red-first rows.

## Verify it headless

```r
shiny::testServer("risk_monitor/shiny", {
  session$setInputs(sig = "ESC-33", level = "red")
  ov()$tiers$Study$score      # rises as the study signal escalates
})
```

## Make it yours

- **Change the rule:** edit `rag_of()` / the tier weights in `R/logic.R`.
- **Change the data:** swap `data/signals.csv` for your KRI feed.
- **Add a tier or a signal:** add rows and (for a new tier) a `TIER_WEIGHT` entry.

Same skeleton as the [lifecycle monitor](../../lifecycle_dashboard/quarto/lifecycle-build.html),
the [UAT tracker](../../uat_irt/quarto/uat-build.html), and the
[find-hours app](../../budget_reconciliation/quarto/hours-build.html).
